info.title: Opentrons HTTP API
info.description: The Opentrons HTTP API is a RESTful JSON-based interface exposed by Opentrons robots (Flex and OT-2) on the local network at port 31950. It enables developers to upload and manage protocols, create and control runs, issue atomic liquid handling commands, and query robot health and hardware state. The API is defined by an OpenAPI specification available directly from the robot at /openapi.json.

robotIP.description: The IP address of the Opentrons robot on the local network

tags.name: Health
tags.description: Robot server health and status endpoints

tags.name: Robot
tags.description: Robot-level control including estop and door status

tags.name: Maintenance Run Management
tags.description: Manage maintenance runs for calibration and hardware control

get.description: Get information about the health of the robot server. Use the health endpoint to check that the robot server is running and ready to operate. A 200 OK response means the server is running. The response includes information about the software and system.

200.description: Robot server is healthy and ready

503.description: Robot motor controller is not ready

post.description: Upload a new protocol file (Python or JSON) to the robot. The protocol will be analyzed after upload.

files.description: Protocol file(s) to upload (Python .py or JSON .json)

runTimeParameterFiles.description: JSON-encoded runtime parameter CSV file associations

protocolKind.description: The kind of protocol being uploaded

get.description: Get a list of all protocols that have been uploaded to the robot.
Get details for a specific uploaded protocol by its ID.

delete.description: Delete a specific protocol from the robot by its ID.

409.description: Protocol is in use by a run

post.description: Trigger a new analysis of a protocol with the given runtime parameter values.

get.description: Get all analyses for a given protocol.

get.summary: Get one of a protocol's analyses
get.description: Get the full details of a specific analysis by its ID.

post.description: Create a new protocol run. A run can be created from an existing protocol or as an empty run for ad-hoc commands.

get.description: Get a list of all runs on the robot.

parameters.name: pageLength
parameters.description: Maximum number of runs to return

get.description: Get the current state of a specific run by its ID.

delete.description: Delete a specific run from the robot.

patch.description: Update properties of an existing run, such as setting it as the current run.

post.summary: Issue a control action to the run
post.description: Issue a control action such as play, pause, stop, or resume-from-recovery to a run.
Add a command to a run. Commands can be setup, protocol, or fixit commands.

parameters.name: waitUntilComplete
parameters.description: If true, wait for the command to complete before returning

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Timeout in milliseconds when waitUntilComplete is true

get.summary: Get a list of all protocol commands in the run
get.description: Get a paginated list of all commands in the run, including their current status.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Index to start returning results from

get.summary: Get full details about a specific command in the run
get.description: Get the complete details of a single command in a run by its ID.

post.summary: Add a command to be executed
post.description: Execute an atomic liquid handling command directly on the robot without needing a run. These stateless commands are executed immediately.

get.summary: Get a list of queued and executed commands
get.description: Get a list of stateless commands that have been issued to the robot.
Get the details of a specific stateless command by its ID.
Get a list of all hardware modules currently attached to the robot, including thermocyclers, heater-shakers, temperature modules, magnetic modules, and absorbance plate readers.
Get a list of all instruments (pipettes and gripper) currently attached to the robot.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.description: If true, refresh the instrument cache before returning

get.description: Get the current deck configuration for a Flex robot, including all fixture assignments.

put.description: Set a new deck configuration for a Flex robot, assigning fixtures to deck slots.

get.description: Get the current system time and date of the robot.

put.description: Set the system time on the robot.

get.description: Get the current status of the emergency stop (estop) button.

put.summary: Acknowledge and clear an Estop event
put.description: Acknowledge that the estop has been disengaged and clear the estop event, allowing normal operation to resume.

get.summary: Get the status of the robot door
get.description: Get the current open/closed status of the robot door.

OTVersionHeader.name: Opentrons-Version
OTVersionHeader.description: The HTTP API version to use for this request. Must be 2 or higher. Use '*' to get the latest version.

api_version.description: The version of the robot's Python API

fw_version.description: The firmware version of the robot's motor controller

board_revision.description: The board revision of the robot

system_version.description: The system (OS) version of the robot

maximum_protocol_api_version.description: The maximum supported Protocol API version [major, minor]

minimum_protocol_api_version.description: The minimum supported Protocol API version [major, minor]

HealthLinks.description: Links to related resources on the robot server

systemTime.description: Path to the system time endpoint

Protocol.description: A protocol uploaded to the robot

role.description: The file's role in the protocol

ProtocolAnalysis.description: The result of analyzing a protocol

runTimeParameterValues.description: Runtime parameter values keyed by parameter variable name

runTimeParameterFiles.description: CSV file IDs keyed by parameter variable name

forceReAnalyze.description: Force a new analysis even if parameters haven't changed

Run.description: A protocol run on the robot

status.description: The current status of the run

current.description: Whether this is the current active run

protocolId.description: The ID of the protocol associated with this run
The ID of the protocol to run

labwareOffsets.description: Labware offsets to apply to this run

runTimeParameterValues.description: Runtime parameter values keyed by variable name

runTimeParameterFiles.description: CSV file IDs keyed by parameter variable name

current.description: Set this run as the current active run

actionType.description: The type of control action to issue

CommandRequest.description: A command to execute on the robot

commandType.description: The type of command to execute (e.g., 'aspirate', 'dispense', 'pickUpTip')

key.description: Optional key to identify this command for error recovery

Command.description: A command that was executed or is queued

Module.description: A hardware module attached to the robot

Instrument.description: A pipette or gripper attached to the robot

mount.description: The mount position of the instrument

CutoutFixture.description: A fixture assigned to a deck cutout on the Flex

opentronsModuleSerialNumber.description: Serial number of the module in this slot, if any

systemTime.description: The current system time in ISO 8601 format
The time to set on the robot

doorRequiredClosedForProtocol.description: Whether the door must be closed to run a protocol

cursor.description: The index of the first item in this page

totalLength.description: The total number of items available
